title: Migrate all database credentials from ConfigMap to Kubernetes Secrets and enforce runtime URL construction
description: Ensure that no database credentials (including passwords) are ever stored in ConfigMaps. Update all service deployments and configuration logic so that POSTGRES_URL in the ConfigMap contains only the host, port, and database name. All passwords must be injected exclusively via Kubernetes Secrets. Each service must construct the full connection URL at runtime by combining the ConfigMap template with the secret-sourced password. Audit all envFrom references and deployment manifests to confirm no credential leakage into ConfigMaps.
subtasks: Refactor ConfigMap sigma1-infra-endpoints to remove any embedded credentials from POSTGRES_URL.
Update all service deployments to inject database passwords via Kubernetes Secrets only.
Modify service startup logic to construct the full connection URL at runtime from ConfigMap and Secret values.
Audit all envFrom and env references in deployment manifests for accidental credential exposure.
Run a cluster-wide scan for any ConfigMaps containing database credentials.
Test all services for successful database connectivity after the change.
Run a security scan to confirm no credentials are present in any ConfigMap.
acceptance_criteria: No ConfigMap in any namespace contains database passwords or credentials.
All services successfully connect to the database using credentials sourced from Kubernetes Secrets.
A scan of all ConfigMaps returns zero matches for database passwords.
All deployment manifests reference only non-secret data in ConfigMaps.
Security scan confirms no credential leakage in environment variables or ConfigMaps.

title: Implement inter-service API key rotation, versioning, and alerting
description: Strengthen inter-service authentication by implementing API key versioning and rotation. Update all services to accept both current and previous API keys during a rotation window. Add a documented rotation procedure to the secret rotation runbook. Generate cryptographically strong (256-bit) keys, store rotation timestamps, and set up alerting for keys not rotated in 90 days. Plan for migration to mTLS in a future phase.
subtasks: Update API key validation logic in all services to support multiple active keys (current and previous) for seamless rotation.
Document and implement a key rotation procedure, including updating secrets and rolling restarts.
Update the secret rotation runbook with the new procedure.
Generate new 256-bit API keys and store rotation timestamps in a secure location.
Set up Prometheus/Grafana alerting for API keys older than 90 days.
Test key rotation by rotating a key and verifying uninterrupted service-to-service communication.
Document the plan for mTLS migration in Phase 2.
acceptance_criteria: All services accept both current and previous API keys during rotation.
Rotation procedure is documented and tested.
API keys are cryptographically strong and have rotation timestamps.
Alert fires if any API key is older than 90 days.
No service downtime occurs during key rotation.
Plan for mTLS migration is documented.

title: Mandate Stripe webhook signature verification for all webhook endpoints
description: Enforce Stripe webhook signature verification in the finance service from the initial implementation. Use the STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET to verify the stripe-signature header on every incoming webhook payload. Reject any request with an invalid or missing signature with a 401 response. This is required to prevent financial fraud.
subtasks: Update the Stripe webhook endpoint to require signature verification using STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
Implement signature validation logic per Stripe's official documentation.
Reject all webhook requests with invalid or missing signatures with a 401 Unauthorized response.
Add unit and integration tests for valid and invalid webhook signatures.
Run an end-to-end test with Stripe's test webhook payloads.
Document the requirement in the service's security documentation.
acceptance_criteria: All Stripe webhook requests are verified using the signing secret.
Requests with invalid or missing signatures are rejected with 401.
Unit and integration tests cover valid and invalid signature scenarios.
End-to-end test with Stripe's test webhook passes.
Documentation clearly states that signature verification is mandatory.

title: Pin all container images to specific digests and enable image scanning in CI/CD
description: Mitigate supply chain and image mutability risks by pinning all container images (including Signal-CLI, Cloudflare Tunnel, Valkey, Rust, and Go base images) to specific SHA256 digests. Audit all Dockerfiles and deployment specs to remove 'latest' or mutable tags. Add image scanning (Trivy or Snyk) to the CI pipeline. Enable a Kubernetes admission policy (Kyverno/OPA) to reject images without digest pinning. Document all base images with their provenance and update schedule.
subtasks: Update all Dockerfiles and Kubernetes manifests to use images pinned by SHA256 digest.
Audit all deployment specs for mutable tags and replace with digests.
Configure image scanning in CI/CD (Trivy or Snyk) for all images.
Enable a Kubernetes admission policy to block images without digest pinning.
Document all base images, their sources, and update schedule.
Test deployments to ensure all images are pulled by digest.
Run a supply chain security scan to verify compliance.
acceptance_criteria: No deployment or Dockerfile uses 'latest' or mutable tags; all are pinned by digest.
CI/CD pipeline fails if image scanning finds critical vulnerabilities.
Kubernetes admission policy blocks unpinned images.
Documentation lists all base images and their provenance.
All deployments succeed with pinned images and pass image scanning.

title: Define and implement user authentication strategy for web frontend and mobile app
description: Resolve the absence of user authentication for the web frontend and mobile app. Either integrate a third-party identity provider (e.g., Auth0, Clerk, Supabase Auth) or explicitly document and harden the system for public, unauthenticated access. If unauthenticated, ensure robust rate limiting on all public endpoints and confirm that no sensitive data is exposed. Align mobile app JWT logic with the chosen authentication approach.
subtasks: Decide and document the authentication strategy: third-party provider integration or public-only endpoints.
If integrating an identity provider, implement user registration, login, session management, and JWT issuance.
Update all API endpoints to require authentication where appropriate.
Align mobile app JWT storage and refresh logic with the chosen provider.
If public, implement and test rate limiting on all public endpoints.
Audit all endpoints to ensure no sensitive data is exposed without authentication.
Update documentation and user flows to reflect the authentication model.
acceptance_criteria: Authentication strategy is documented and implemented.
All endpoints requiring authentication enforce it.
Mobile app JWT logic matches backend expectations.
Rate limiting is active and effective on public endpoints.
No sensitive data is accessible without authentication.
Documentation is updated to reflect the current model.

title: Implement GDPR deletion request queue with customer identity verification and admin approval
description: Redesign the GDPR deletion workflow to require customer identity verification and admin approval before any deletion is executed. Implement a request queue: customers submit deletion requests through an authenticated channel, requests are stored with status 'pending_verification', an identity verification step is performed (e.g., email confirmation or ID check), and admin approval is required before the orchestrator executes the deletion. The orchestrator must only process verified and approved requests. Add rate limiting to all GDPR endpoints to prevent abuse.
subtasks: Implement a GDPR deletion request queue (e.g., audit.gdpr_deletion_requests table) with status tracking.
Require all deletion requests to be submitted via an authenticated channel.
Add an identity verification step (e.g., email confirmation link or manual ID check).
Require explicit admin (Mike) approval before processing any deletion.
Update the orchestrator to process only verified and approved requests.
Add rate limiting to all GDPR endpoints.
Test the full workflow: request, verification, approval, execution.
acceptance_criteria: No GDPR deletion is processed without verified customer identity and admin approval.
All requests are tracked with status and audit log.
Rate limiting is enforced on all GDPR endpoints.
End-to-end test demonstrates the full workflow.
Documentation reflects the new process.

title: Implement data minimization and retention policy for vetting service raw API responses
description: Apply data minimization to the vetting service: define which fields from each API response are needed for audit, strip unnecessary PII before storing raw_responses, and apply GDPR deletion to raw_responses. Consider encrypting the raw_responses column at rest and set a retention policy (e.g., 12 months max).
subtasks: Define and document the minimal set of fields required from each external API for audit purposes.
Implement logic to redact or exclude unnecessary PII (e.g., director home addresses, personal emails) before storing in raw_responses.
Update GDPR deletion logic to ensure raw_responses are deleted or anonymized as part of customer deletion.
Evaluate and, if feasible, implement encryption at rest for the raw_responses column (e.g., pgcrypto).
Set and enforce a retention policy for raw_responses (e.g., scheduled deletion after 12 months).
Test that only the minimal required data is stored and that GDPR deletions remove all relevant data.
acceptance_criteria: Only minimal, documented fields are stored in raw_responses.
No unnecessary PII is retained.
GDPR deletion removes or anonymizes all relevant data.
Retention policy is enforced and documented.
Encryption at rest is implemented or a documented decision is made.

title: Enforce TLS for all PgBouncer and PostgreSQL connections
description: Enable TLS on the PgBouncer Pooler and PostgreSQL using CloudNativePG's built-in TLS support. Ensure all POSTGRES_URL connection strings include sslmode=require or sslmode=verify-full. Update CloudNativePG configuration to enforce TLS connections. Verify Valkey has TLS enabled or document as a Phase 2 item.
subtasks: Update PgBouncer Pooler CR to enable TLS using CloudNativePG's TLS settings.
Update all service connection strings to include sslmode=require or sslmode=verify-full.
Update pg_hba.conf to require hostssl for all connections.
Test all service connections over TLS.
Document the status of Valkey TLS support and plan for Phase 2 if not feasible now.
acceptance_criteria: All database connections between services, PgBouncer, and PostgreSQL are encrypted with TLS.
All connection strings use sslmode=require or sslmode=verify-full.
pg_hba.conf enforces hostssl for all connections.
Valkey TLS status is documented and planned.
All services pass connectivity and encryption tests.

title: Define and enforce WebSocket chat endpoint authentication and rate limiting
description: Define and implement a concrete authentication flow for the Morgan WebSocket chat endpoint. Require a short-lived session token or API key on connection handshake, validate the token server-side, and implement per-connection rate limiting. Sanitize all user input before passing to Morgan's LLM to prevent prompt injection.
subtasks: Implement an authenticated HTTP endpoint to generate short-lived session tokens.
Require the session token as a query parameter or in the first WebSocket message.
Validate the token server-side before processing any messages.
Implement per-connection rate limiting (e.g., messages per minute).
Sanitize all user input before passing to the LLM.
Test authentication and rate limiting with valid and invalid tokens.
Document the authentication flow for frontend and mobile clients.
acceptance_criteria: WebSocket connections require a valid session token or API key.
Invalid or expired tokens are rejected.
Rate limiting is enforced per connection.
All user input is sanitized before LLM processing.
Documentation is updated for client integration.

title: Implement prompt injection protections and tool-level authorization for Morgan AI agent
description: Implement defense-in-depth against prompt injection for the Morgan AI agent. Add an input sanitization layer before text reaches the LLM, require tool-level authorization for sensitive actions (e.g., GDPR delete, invoice creation), filter outputs to prevent leaking internal details, rate limit tool calls per session, log all tool invocations with context, and implement a 'confirmed action' pattern for destructive actions.
subtasks: Implement input sanitization for all user-supplied text before LLM processing.
Require explicit user confirmation for destructive tool calls (e.g., GDPR delete, invoice creation).
Add tool-level authorization checks for sensitive actions.
Implement output filtering to prevent leaking internal system details.
Rate limit tool calls per conversation session.
Log all tool invocations with conversation context for audit.
Test prompt injection scenarios and verify protections are effective.
acceptance_criteria: Input sanitization is active for all user input.
Sensitive tool calls require explicit confirmation and authorization.
Output filtering prevents internal detail leakage.
Tool call rate limiting is enforced per session.
All tool invocations are logged with context.
Prompt injection tests do not result in unauthorized tool calls or data access.

title: Add security-focused alerting and anomaly detection to monitoring stack
description: Enhance observability by adding Prometheus alert rules for security events: high rate of 401/403 responses, GDPR deletion events, unusual tool call patterns from Morgan, high-volume Signal-CLI sends, and unexpected database connections. Create a dedicated security events Grafana dashboard.
subtasks: Define and implement Prometheus alert rules for high 401/403 rates, GDPR deletion events, unusual tool call patterns, high-volume Signal-CLI sends, and unexpected DB connections.
Update the monitoring stack to include a security events Grafana dashboard.
Test alerting by simulating each security event and verifying alert delivery.
Document all new alert rules and dashboard panels.
acceptance_criteria: Prometheus alerts fire for all defined security events.
Security events dashboard displays real-time and historical data.
Alerts are delivered to the appropriate notification channels.
Documentation is updated with all new rules and dashboards.
